16	from .utils import (
17	    _maybe_fake_quantize_activations,
18	    fast_dequantize,
19	    QUANT_STATE,
20	    get_lora_parameters,

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass

465	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result
543	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",

1826	    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit

1839	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1840	            python_install.wait()
1841	            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)

1853	        try:
1854	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1855	            makefile = None

1857	            # Retry by recloning llama.cpp

I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me compile the complete report.

---

Here is the complete analysis of the unsloth codebase.

## Repo File Tree

```
unsloth/                         (root)
├── .github/

│   ├── test_model_registry.py
│   └── utils/
├── unsloth/
│   ├── __init__.py              # top-level package; re-exports from .models, .save, .chat_templates, .trainer
│   ├── _auto_install.py
│   ├── chat_templates.py        # calls patch_saving_functions(tokenizer)
│   ├── import_fixes.py
│   ├── save.py                  # ALL save/export logic (2678 lines)
│   ├── tokenizer_utils.py
│   ├── trainer.py
│   ├── dataprep/
│   ├── kernels/
│   │   ├── __init__.py

│   │   ├── geglu.py
│   │   ├── layernorm.py
│   │   ├── moe/
│   │   ├── rms_layernorm.py

│   │   └── utils.py             # get_lora_parameters, get_lora_parameters_bias
│   ├── models/

│   │   ├── _utils.py

│   │   ├── llama4.py

│   │   ├── loader_utils.py

```

---

## Save / Export Methods: Exact File Paths and Line Numbers

All save/export logic lives in `/private/var/folders/vq/.../unsloth/save.py`.

### Core saving engine
| Function | Line | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `unsloth_save_model` | 205 | Main workhorse — called by nearly everything. `save_method` can be `"lora"`, `"merged_16bit"`, or `"merged_4bit"`. |
| `unsloth_generic_save` | 2356 | Wrapper version used by the `FastBaseModel`/`FastModel` code path. |

### Methods dynamically patched onto every model instance (via `patch_saving_functions`, line 2569)

For **language models** (`vision=False`):

| Method name (on model object) | Backing function | Line |
|---|---|---|
| `model.save_pretrained_merged` | `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged` | 2413 |
| `model.push_to_hub_merged` | `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged` | 2456 |
| `model.save_pretrained_gguf` | `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf` | 1768 |
| `model.push_to_hub_gguf` | `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf` | 1951 |
| `model.save_pretrained_torchao` | `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` | 2502 |
| `model.save_pretrained_ggml` | `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally` | 2211 |
| `model.push_to_hub_ggml` | `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub` | 2143 |
| `model.push_to_hub` (monkey-patched) | `unsloth_push_to_hub` (exec'd dynamically) | ~2586 |

## LoRA A/B Matrix Storage

The LoRA adapters follow standard PEFT conventions. Each linear layer that has LoRA applied is wrapped in a `Peft_Linear` or `Peft_Linear4bit` object with these attributes:

**Attribute structure on a LoRA-wrapped layer (`proj`):**
```
proj.base_layer          -> the original nn.Linear / Bnb_Linear4bit
proj.base_layer.weight   -> base weight tensor W
proj.lora_A              -> dict[str, nn.Linear]  keyed by adapter name (default: "default")
proj.lora_B              -> dict[str, nn.Linear]  keyed by adapter name
proj.lora_A["default"].weight  -> the A matrix  (shape: [rank, in_features])
proj.lora_B["default"].weight  -> the B matrix  (shape: [out_features, rank])
proj.scaling             -> dict[str, float]  the lora_alpha/r scaling factor
proj.active_adapters     -> list of active adapter names, e.g. ["default"]
```

The access pattern used consistently in `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190–231):
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    adapter = proj.active_adapters[0]      # or active_adapter
    A = proj.lora_A[adapter].weight        # line 218
    B = proj.lora_B[adapter].weight        # line 219
    S = proj.scaling[adapter]              # line 230
    return W, quant_state, A, B, S
```

The LoRA update formula is `W_effective = W + A.T @ (S * B.T)` (note: weights are stored transposed relative to the math — `A` shape `[rank, in]`, `B` shape `[out, rank]`).

Weight names in the parameter namespace follow the pattern `.lora_A.` and `.lora_B.` (checked at `/unsloth/models/gemma.py` line 383, `/unsloth/models/gemma2.py` line 506).

The primary user-facing entrypoints documented/used in the wild are:

2. `FastModel.from_pretrained(...)` + `FastModel.get_peft_model(...)` — the new unified path (handles vision + language + text-to-speech)

The `export_to_onnx` method should be added as a **standalone function** in `/unsloth/save.py` (alongside `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf`, `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao`, etc.), then wired into `patch_saving_functions` (line 2657–2676) so it gets dynamically bound to every model instance as `model.save_pretrained_onnx`.

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )

Key structural observations:

- **`__all__`** (lines 48–54) exports exactly 5 names: `print_quantization_methods`, `unsloth_save_model`, `save_to_gguf`, `patch_saving_functions`, `create_huggingface_repo`. Notably `get_lora_parameters` is NOT in `__all__` for `save.py` — it lives in `kernels/utils.py` and is imported via `.kernels` as `get_lora_parameters_bias` (line 29).

- **`patch_saving_functions`** (lines 2569–2678) works by: (1) introspecting the model's existing `push_to_hub` signature with `inspect.signature`, (2) building a full function definition string `push_to_hub_text` at runtime, (3) calling `exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())` to inject `unsloth_push_to_hub` into globals, (4) walking the `model.model` chain to bind it with `types.MethodType`, then (5) attaching all the save/push methods directly onto the model object. Vision mode skips the GGUF/GGML methods and uses `save_to_gguf_generic` stubs instead.

- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao`** (lines 2502–2561) calls `unsloth_generic_save` first (forcing `save_method="merged_16bit"` and `push_to_hub=False`), then reloads the merged model with `TorchAoConfig`, quantizes, and saves to a directory suffixed `-torchao`.

- **`get_lora_parameters`** returns a 5-tuple `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`. It handles QAT via optional `weight_fake_quantizer` attributes, and returns `(W, quant_state, None, None, None)` when adapters are disabled or merged.

---

Key structural observations:

- **`__all__`** (lines 48–54) exports exactly 5 names: `print_quantization_methods`, `unsloth_save_model`, `save_to_gguf`, `patch_saving_functions`, `create_huggingface_repo`. Notably `get_lora_parameters` is NOT in `__all__` for `save.py` — it lives in `kernels/utils.py` and is imported via `.kernels` as `get_lora_parameters_bias` (line 29).

- **`patch_saving_functions`** (lines 2569–2678) works by: (1) introspecting the model's existing `push_to_hub` signature with `inspect.signature`, (2) building a full function definition string `push_to_hub_text` at runtime, (3) calling `exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())` to inject `unsloth_push_to_hub` into globals, (4) walking the `model.model` chain to bind it with `types.MethodType`, then (5) attaching all the save/push methods directly onto the model object. Vision mode skips the GGUF/GGML methods and uses `save_to_gguf_generic` stubs instead.

- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao`** (lines 2502–2561) calls `unsloth_generic_save` first (forcing `save_method="merged_16bit"` and `push_to_hub=False`), then reloads the merged model with `TorchAoConfig`, quantizes, and saves to a directory suffixed `-torchao`.

- **`get_lora_parameters`** returns a 5-tuple `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`. It handles QAT via optional `weight_fake_quantizer` attributes, and returns `(W, quant_state, None, None, None)` when adapters are disabled or merged.

input.content:
# Plan: Add `export_to_onnx` / `save_pretrained_onnx` to Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth exposes a family of `model.save_pretrained_*` methods (GGUF, GGML, merged 16-bit,
torchao) by dynamically binding functions onto the model in `patch_saving_functions`.
The goal is to add a new `model.save_pretrained_onnx(save_directory, opset_version=17)`
method that exports each LoRA adapter's A and B weight matrices to individual ONNX files
(one per adapted module), plus a `metadata.json` file containing rank, alpha, and
target_modules. The implementation follows the exact patterns already used by
`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` and the torchao binding in `patch_saving_functions`.

---

## Critical file

**Only one file is modified:** `unsloth/save.py`

---

## Step-by-step implementation

### Step 1 — Add `import json` (line ~27)

`json` is not yet imported in `save.py`. Insert it after the existing stdlib imports:

```python
# after:  import gc
import json
```

### Step 2 — Import `get_lora_parameters` from `.kernels` (line 29)

`save.py` line 29 currently imports only `get_lora_parameters_bias`.
Add `get_lora_parameters` to the same import:

```python
# before:
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias

# after:
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters, get_lora_parameters_bias
```

`get_lora_parameters` is already exported from `unsloth/kernels/__init__.py` line 38.
It returns `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)` and handles quantised/QAT layers cleanly.

### Step 3 — Add `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` to `__all__` (lines 48–54)

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx",   # ← add this line
]
```

### Step 4 — Add `_LoRAONNXModule` helper class and `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx`

Insert after `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` ends (line ~2561, before `not_implemented_save`).

#### 4a. `_LoRAONNXModule`

A minimal `nn.Module` whose sole purpose is to represent the LoRA delta computation
so `torch.onnx.export` can trace it. The A and B matrices are registered as buffers
(not parameters) so they appear as ONNX initializers.

```python
class _LoRAONNXModule(torch.nn.Module):
    """Wraps a single LoRA adapter (one A + B pair) as a traceable nn.Module.

    Forward computes the LoRA delta:
        lora_delta = (x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T * scaling

    Shapes:
        lora_A : [rank, in_features]
        lora_B : [out_features, rank]
        x      : [batch, in_features]   (dummy at export time, batch=1)
        output : [batch, out_features]
    """
    def __init__(self, lora_A, lora_B, scaling):
        super().__init__()
        self.register_buffer("lora_A", lora_A.detach().float())
        self.register_buffer("lora_B", lora_B.detach().float())
        self.scaling = float(scaling)

    def forward(self, x):
        return (x @ self.lora_A.t()) @ self.lora_B.t() * self.scaling
```

Notes:
- `.detach()` prevents autograd from following into the original model.
- Cast to `float()` (float32) for maximum ONNX compatibility; bfloat16/int4 tensors
  are not supported by all ONNX opsets.
- `self.scaling` is a plain Python float — it will be folded as a constant node
  by the ONNX exporter.

#### 4b. `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx`

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version        : int = 17,
):
    """Exports LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    For each LoRA-adapted linear module one ONNX file is written:
        <save_directory>/<module_name>.onnx

    The ONNX graph encodes the LoRA delta computation:
        lora_delta = (x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T * scaling

    A metadata file is also written:
        <save_directory>/metadata.json

    containing rank, lora_alpha, target_modules, opset_version, adapter_name,
    and per-module details (in_features, out_features, rank, scaling, onnx_file).

    Args:
        save_directory  : Local folder path. Created if it does not exist.
        opset_version   : ONNX opset version (default 17).
    """
    import torch.onnx

    save_directory = str(save_directory)
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # ── 1. Extract LoRA adapter config ───────────────────────────────────────
    peft_cfg      = getattr(self, "peft_config", {})
    adapter_name  = next(iter(peft_cfg), "default")
    lora_cfg      = peft_cfg.get(adapter_name, None)

    rank           = int(getattr(lora_cfg, "r",            0))  if lora_cfg else 0
    lora_alpha     = float(getattr(lora_cfg, "lora_alpha", 0))  if lora_cfg else 0.0
    target_modules = sorted(getattr(lora_cfg, "target_modules", []) or [])

    # ── 2. Iterate modules, export each LoRA layer ───────────────────────────
    modules_meta  = {}
    exported      = 0

    for module_name, module in self.named_modules():
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue

        _, _, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(module)
        if A is None or B is None:
            # Adapters disabled or already merged — skip
            continue

        in_features  = A.shape[1]   # A: [rank, in_features]
        out_features = B.shape[0]   # B: [out_features, rank]
        r            = A.shape[0]   # actual rank (may differ per-layer)

        onnx_module  = _LoRAONNXModule(A, B, scaling)
        onnx_module.eval()

        dummy_input  = torch.zeros(1, in_features, dtype=torch.float32)
        onnx_filename = module_name + ".onnx"
        onnx_path     = os.path.join(save_directory, onnx_filename)

        torch.onnx.export(
            onnx_module,
            (dummy_input,),
            onnx_path,
            input_names   = ["x"],
            output_names  = ["lora_delta"],
            opset_version = opset_version,
            do_constant_folding = True,
        )

        modules_meta[module_name] = {
            "onnx_file"   : onnx_filename,
            "in_features" : in_features,
            "out_features": out_features,
            "rank"        : r,
            "scaling"     : float(scaling),
        }
        exported += 1
    pass

    if exported == 0:
        logger.warning(
            "Unsloth: No LoRA adapter modules found to export. "
            "Make sure the model has active (un-merged) LoRA adapters."
        )

    # ── 3. Write metadata.json ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    metadata = {
        "adapter_name"   : adapter_name,
        "rank"           : rank,
        "lora_alpha"     : lora_alpha,
        "target_modules" : target_modules,
        "opset_version"  : opset_version,
        "modules"        : modules_meta,
    }
    metadata_path = os.path.join(save_directory, "metadata.json")
    with open(metadata_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)

    logger.warning_once(
        f"Unsloth: Exported {exported} LoRA adapter(s) to ONNX in '{save_directory}'.\n"
        f"Metadata written to '{metadata_path}'."
    )
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass
```

### Step 5 — Wire into `patch_saving_functions` (lines 2657–2675)

In `patch_saving_functions`, add one binding in **each** branch.

**Non-vision branch** (after existing line 2664):
```python
# existing:

# add:

```

**Vision branch** (after existing line 2674):
```python

# add:
model.save_pretrained_onnx    = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,    model)
```

LoRA adapters behave identically for vision and language models, so both branches
get the method (unlike GGUF which is language-only).

---

## Design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| One ONNX file per adapted module | ✓ | Files are self-contained; can be loaded/run independently by downstream tools |
| Matrices cast to float32 | ✓ | bfloat16/int4 not universally supported across ONNX runtimes |
| `do_constant_folding=True` | ✓ | Folds `scaling` scalar into the graph at export time |
| `metadata.json` (not pickle) | ✓ | Human-readable; interoperable with non-Python tools |
| Scaling stored per-module in JSON | ✓ | Some models use per-layer rank/alpha (DoRA, rank-stabilised) |
| Bind to both vision and non-vision | ✓ | LoRA adapters are architecture-agnostic |
| `A` and `B` as ONNX initializers (buffers) | ✓ | Shows up as named constants in the graph, easy for runtime inspection |

---

#### 4a. `_LoRAONNXModule`

A minimal `nn.Module` whose sole purpose is to represent the LoRA delta computation

```python
class _LoRAONNXModule(torch.nn.Module):
    """Wraps a single LoRA adapter (one A + B pair) as a traceable nn.Module.

    Forward computes the LoRA delta:
        lora_delta = (x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T * scaling

    Shapes:

        lora_B : [out_features, rank]

        output : [batch, out_features]

    def __init__(self, lora_A, lora_B, scaling):
        super().__init__()
        self.register_buffer("lora_A", lora_A.detach().float())
        self.register_buffer("lora_B", lora_B.detach().float())
        self.scaling = float(scaling)

    def forward(self, x):
        return (x @ self.lora_A.t()) @ self.lora_B.t() * self.scaling
```

- `self.scaling` is a plain Python float — it will be folded as a constant node

- Cast to `float()` (float32) for maximum ONNX compatibility; bfloat16/int4 tensors
  are not supported by all ONNX opsets.
- `self.scaling` is a plain Python float — it will be folded as a constant node
  by the ONNX exporter.

#### 4b. `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx`

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version        : int = 17,
):
    """Exports LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    For each LoRA-adapted linear module one ONNX file is written:
        <save_directory>/<module_name>.onnx

    The ONNX graph encodes the LoRA delta computation:
        lora_delta = (x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T * scaling

    A metadata file is also written:
        <save_directory>/metadata.json

    containing rank, lora_alpha, target_modules, opset_version, adapter_name,
    and per-module details (in_features, out_features, rank, scaling, onnx_file).

    Args:
        save_directory  : Local folder path. Created if it does not exist.
        opset_version   : ONNX opset version (default 17).
    """
    import torch.onnx

    save_directory = str(save_directory)
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # ── 1. Extract LoRA adapter config ───────────────────────────────────────
    peft_cfg      = getattr(self, "peft_config", {})
    adapter_name  = next(iter(peft_cfg), "default")
    lora_cfg      = peft_cfg.get(adapter_name, None)

    rank           = int(getattr(lora_cfg, "r",            0))  if lora_cfg else 0
    lora_alpha     = float(getattr(lora_cfg, "lora_alpha", 0))  if lora_cfg else 0.0
    target_modules = sorted(getattr(lora_cfg, "target_modules", []) or [])

    # ── 2. Iterate modules, export each LoRA layer ───────────────────────────
    modules_meta  = {}
    exported      = 0

    for module_name, module in self.named_modules():
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue

        _, _, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(module)
        if A is None or B is None:
            # Adapters disabled or already merged — skip
            continue

        in_features  = A.shape[1]   # A: [rank, in_features]
        out_features = B.shape[0]   # B: [out_features, rank]
        r            = A.shape[0]   # actual rank (may differ per-layer)

        onnx_module  = _LoRAONNXModule(A, B, scaling)
        onnx_module.eval()

        dummy_input  = torch.zeros(1, in_features, dtype=torch.float32)
        onnx_filename = module_name + ".onnx"
        onnx_path     = os.path.join(save_directory, onnx_filename)

        torch.onnx.export(
            onnx_module,
            (dummy_input,),
            onnx_path,
            input_names   = ["x"],
            output_names  = ["lora_delta"],
            opset_version = opset_version,
            do_constant_folding = True,
        )

        modules_meta[module_name] = {
            "onnx_file"   : onnx_filename,
            "in_features" : in_features,
            "out_features": out_features,
            "rank"        : r,
            "scaling"     : float(scaling),
        }
        exported += 1
    pass

    if exported == 0:
        logger.warning(
            "Unsloth: No LoRA adapter modules found to export. "
            "Make sure the model has active (un-merged) LoRA adapters."
        )

    # ── 3. Write metadata.json ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    metadata = {
        "adapter_name"   : adapter_name,
        "rank"           : rank,
        "lora_alpha"     : lora_alpha,
        "target_modules" : target_modules,
        "opset_version"  : opset_version,
        "modules"        : modules_meta,
    }
    metadata_path = os.path.join(save_directory, "metadata.json")
    with open(metadata_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)

    logger.warning_once(
        f"Unsloth: Exported {exported} LoRA adapter(s) to ONNX in '{save_directory}'.\n"
        f"Metadata written to '{metadata_path}'."
    )
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass
```

### Step 5 — Wire into `patch_saving_functions` (lines 2657–2675)

In `patch_saving_functions`, add one binding in **each** branch.

**Non-vision branch** (after existing line 2664):
```python
# existing:
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
# add:
model.save_pretrained_onnx    = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,    model)
```

**Vision branch** (after existing line 2674):
```python
# existing:
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
# add:
model.save_pretrained_onnx    = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,    model)
```

LoRA adapters behave identically for vision and language models, so both branches
get the method (unlike GGUF which is language-only).

---

## Design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| One ONNX file per adapted module | ✓ | Files are self-contained; can be loaded/run independently by downstream tools |
| Matrices cast to float32 | ✓ | bfloat16/int4 not universally supported across ONNX runtimes |
| `do_constant_folding=True` | ✓ | Folds `scaling` scalar into the graph at export time |
| `metadata.json` (not pickle) | ✓ | Human-readable; interoperable with non-Python tools |
| Scaling stored per-module in JSON | ✓ | Some models use per-layer rank/alpha (DoRA, rank-stabilised) |
| Bind to both vision and non-vision | ✓ | LoRA adapters are architecture-agnostic |
| `A` and `B` as ONNX initializers (buffers) | ✓ | Shows up as named constants in the graph, easy for runtime inspection |

---

## Summary of all edits to `unsloth/save.py`

| Location | Change |
|---|---|
| Line ~27 | Add `import json` |
| Line 29 | Add `get_lora_parameters` to the `.kernels` import |
| Lines 48–54 | Add `"unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx"` to `__all__` |
| After line ~2561 | Insert `_LoRAONNXModule` class |
| After line ~2561 | Insert `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` function |
| Line ~2664 (non-vision) | Add `model.save_pretrained_onnx` binding |
| Line ~2674 (vision) | Add `model.save_pretrained_onnx` binding |

---

## Verification

After implementation, verify with a minimal test (no install needed — just read the source):

1. **Static check**: Confirm `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` appears in `__all__` and
   is referenced in both branches of `patch_saving_functions`.

2. **Runtime test sketch** (for human tester with the environment):
   ```python
   from unsloth import FastLanguageModel
   model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained("unsloth/tinyllama-bnb-4bit")
   model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, r=4, target_modules=["q_proj","v_proj"])
   # train briefly or skip training
   model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_lora_onnx", opset_version=17)
   # Expected:
   # - my_lora_onnx/metadata.json  (contains rank=4, target_modules=[...])
   # - my_lora_onnx/base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.onnx
   # - my_lora_onnx/base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.v_proj.onnx
   # - ... (one .onnx per adapted layer)
   import json, onnx
   meta = json.load(open("my_lora_onnx/metadata.json"))
   assert meta["rank"] == 4
   assert "q_proj" in meta["target_modules"]
   # Load one ONNX file and check graph inputs/outputs
   m = onnx.load(list(meta["modules"].values())[0]["onnx_file"])
   assert m.graph.input[0].name == "x"
   assert m.graph.output[0].name == "lora_delta"
   ```

3. **Attribute existence check** (pure static — no runtime):
   Grep for `save_pretrained_onnx` in `save.py` — must appear in 3 places:
   the function definition, the non-vision binding, and the vision binding.

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `export_to_onnx` / `save_pretrained_onnx` to Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth exposes a family of `model.save_pretrained_*` methods (GGUF, GGML, merged 16-bit,
torchao) by dynamically binding functions onto the model in `patch_saving_functions`.
The goal is to add a new `model.save_pretrained_onnx(save_directory, opset_version=17)`
method that exports each LoRA adapter's A and B weight matrices to individual ONNX files
(one per adapted module), plus a `metadata.json` file containing rank, alpha, and
target_modules. The implementation follows the exact patterns already used by
`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` and the torchao binding in `patch_saving_functions`.

---

## Critical file

**Only one file is modified:** `unsloth/save.py`

---

## Step-by-step implementation

### Step 1 — Add `import json` (line ~27)

`json` is not yet imported in `save.py`. Insert it after the existing stdlib imports:

```python
# after:  import gc
import json
```

### Step 2 — Import `get_lora_parameters` from `.kernels` (line 29)

`save.py` line 29 currently imports only `get_lora_parameters_bias`.
Add `get_lora_parameters` to the same import:

```python
# before:
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias

# after:
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters, get_lora_parameters_bias
```

`get_lora_parameters` is already exported from `unsloth/kernels/__init__.py` line 38.
It returns `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)` and handles quantised/QAT layers cleanly.

### Step 3 — Add `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` to `__all__` (lines 48–54)

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx",   # ← add this line
]
```

### Step 4 — Add `_LoRAONNXModule` helper class and `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx`

Insert after `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` ends (line ~2561, before `not_implemented_save`).

#### 4a. `_LoRAONNXModule`

A minimal `nn.Module` whose sole purpose is to represent the LoRA delta computation
so `torch.onnx.export` can trace it. The A and B matrices are registered as buffers
(not parameters) so they appear as ONNX initializers.

```python
class _LoRAONNXModule(torch.nn.Module):
    """Wraps a single LoRA adapter (one A + B pair) as a traceable nn.Module.

    Forward computes the LoRA delta:
        lora_delta = (x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T * scaling

    Shapes:
        lora_A : [rank, in_features]
        lora_B : [out_features, rank]
        x      : [batch, in_features]   (dummy at export time, batch=1)
        output : [batch, out_features]
    """
    def __init__(self, lora_A, lora_B, scaling):
        super().__init__()
        self.register_buffer("lora_A", lora_A.detach().float())
        self.register_buffer("lora_B", lora_B.detach().float())
        self.scaling = float(scaling)

    def forward(self, x):
        return (x @ self.lora_A.t()) @ self.lora_B.t() * self.scaling
```

Notes:
- `.detach()` prevents autograd from following into the original model.
- Cast to `float()` (float32) for maximum ONNX compatibility; bfloat16/int4 tensors
  are not supported by all ONNX opsets.
- `self.scaling` is a plain Python float — it will be folded as a constant node
  by the ONNX exporter.

#### 4b. `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx`

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version        : int = 17,
):
    """Exports LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

    For each LoRA-adapted linear module one ONNX file is written:
        <save_directory>/<module_name>.onnx

    The ONNX graph encodes the LoRA delta computation:
        lora_delta = (x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T * scaling

    A metadata file is also written:
        <save_directory>/metadata.json

    containing rank, lora_alpha, target_modules, opset_version, adapter_name,
    and per-module details (in_features, out_features, rank, scaling, onnx_file).

    Args:
        save_directory  : Local folder path. Created if it does not exist.
        opset_version   : ONNX opset version (default 17).
    """
    import torch.onnx

    save_directory = str(save_directory)
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # ── 1. Extract LoRA adapter config ───────────────────────────────────────
    peft_cfg      = getattr(self, "peft_config", {})
    adapter_name  = next(iter(peft_cfg), "default")
    lora_cfg      = peft_cfg.get(adapter_name, None)

    rank           = int(getattr(lora_cfg, "r",            0))  if lora_cfg else 0
    lora_alpha     = float(getattr(lora_cfg, "lora_alpha", 0))  if lora_cfg else 0.0
    target_modules = sorted(getattr(lora_cfg, "target_modules", []) or [])

    # ── 2. Iterate modules, export each LoRA layer ───────────────────────────
    modules_meta  = {}
    exported      = 0

    for module_name, module in self.named_modules():
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue

        _, _, A, B, scaling = get_lora_parameters(module)
        if A is None or B is None:
            # Adapters disabled or already merged — skip
            continue

        in_features  = A.shape[1]   # A: [rank, in_features]
        out_features = B.shape[0]   # B: [out_features, rank]
        r            = A.shape[0]   # actual rank (may differ per-layer)

        onnx_module  = _LoRAONNXModule(A, B, scaling)
        onnx_module.eval()

        dummy_input  = torch.zeros(1, in_features, dtype=torch.float32)
        onnx_filename = module_name + ".onnx"
        onnx_path     = os.path.join(save_directory, onnx_filename)

        torch.onnx.export(
            onnx_module,
            (dummy_input,),
            onnx_path,
            input_names   = ["x"],
            output_names  = ["lora_delta"],
            opset_version = opset_version,
            do_constant_folding = True,
        )

        modules_meta[module_name] = {
            "onnx_file"   : onnx_filename,
            "in_features" : in_features,
            "out_features": out_features,
            "rank"        : r,
            "scaling"     : float(scaling),
        }
        exported += 1
    pass

    if exported == 0:
        logger.warning(
            "Unsloth: No LoRA adapter modules found to export. "
            "Make sure the model has active (un-merged) LoRA adapters."
        )

    # ── 3. Write metadata.json ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    metadata = {
        "adapter_name"   : adapter_name,
        "rank"           : rank,
        "lora_alpha"     : lora_alpha,
        "target_modules" : target_modules,
        "opset_version"  : opset_version,
        "modules"        : modules_meta,
    }
    metadata_path = os.path.join(save_directory, "metadata.json")
    with open(metadata_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)

    logger.warning_once(
        f"Unsloth: Exported {exported} LoRA adapter(s) to ONNX in '{save_directory}'.\n"
        f"Metadata written to '{metadata_path}'."
    )
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass
```

### Step 5 — Wire into `patch_saving_functions` (lines 2657–2675)

In `patch_saving_functions`, add one binding in **each** branch.

**Non-vision branch** (after existing line 2664):
```python
# existing:
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
# add:
model.save_pretrained_onnx    = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,    model)
```

**Vision branch** (after existing line 2674):
```python
# existing:
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
# add:
model.save_pretrained_onnx    = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,    model)
```

LoRA adapters behave identically for vision and language models, so both branches
get the method (unlike GGUF which is language-only).

---

## Design decisions

| Decision | Choice | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| One ONNX file per adapted module | ✓ | Files are self-contained; can be loaded/run independently by downstream tools |
| Matrices cast to float32 | ✓ | bfloat16/int4 not universally supported across ONNX runtimes |
| `do_constant_folding=True` | ✓ | Folds `scaling` scalar into the graph at export time |
| `metadata.json` (not pickle) | ✓ | Human-readable; interoperable with non-Python tools |
| Scaling stored per-module in JSON | ✓ | Some models use per-layer rank/alpha (DoRA, rank-stabilised) |
| Bind to both vision and non-vision | ✓ | LoRA adapters are architecture-agnostic |
| `A` and `B` as ONNX initializers (buffers) | ✓ | Shows up as named constants in the graph, easy for runtime inspection |

---

## Summary of all edits to `unsloth/save.py`

| Location | Change |

| Line ~27 | Add `import json` |
| Line 29 | Add `get_lora_parameters` to the `.kernels` import |
| Lines 48–54 | Add `"unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx"` to `__all__` |
| After line ~2561 | Insert `_LoRAONNXModule` class |
| After line ~2561 | Insert `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` function |
| Line ~2664 (non-vision) | Add `model.save_pretrained_onnx` binding |
| Line ~2674 (vision) | Add `model.save_pretrained_onnx` binding |

---

## Verification

After implementation, verify with a minimal test (no install needed — just read the source):

1. **Static check**: Confirm `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` appears in `__all__` and
   is referenced in both branches of `patch_saving_functions`.

2. **Runtime test sketch** (for human tester with the environment):
   ```python

   model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained("unsloth/tinyllama-bnb-4bit")
   model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, r=4, target_modules=["q_proj","v_proj"])
   # train briefly or skip training
   model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_lora_onnx", opset_version=17)
   # Expected:
   # - my_lora_onnx/metadata.json  (contains rank=4, target_modules=[...])
   # - my_lora_onnx/base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.onnx
   # - my_lora_onnx/base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.v_proj.onnx
   # - ... (one .onnx per adapted layer)
   import json, onnx
   meta = json.load(open("my_lora_onnx/metadata.json"))
   assert meta["rank"] == 4
   assert "q_proj" in meta["target_modules"]
   # Load one ONNX file and check graph inputs/outputs
   m = onnx.load(list(meta["modules"].values())[0]["onnx_file"])
   assert m.graph.input[0].name == "x"
   assert m.graph.output[0].name == "lora_delta"
   ```

3. **Attribute existence check** (pure static — no runtime):
   Grep for `save_pretrained_onnx` in `save.py` — must appear in 3 places:
   the function definition, the non-vision binding, and the vision binding.

Here is the complete implementation plan. Only **one file** needs to be modified: `unsloth/save.py`.

---

### What gets built

`model.save_pretrained_onnx(save_directory, opset_version=17)` — exposed alongside the existing `save_pretrained_gguf`, `save_pretrained_merged`, and `save_pretrained_torchao` methods.

**Output per call:**
- One `.onnx` file per LoRA-adapted module (e.g. `base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.onnx`), each encoding the delta computation `(x @ A.T) @ B.T * scaling`
- One `metadata.json` — contains `rank`, `lora_alpha`, `target_modules`, `opset_version`, `adapter_name`, and per-module details

---

### 7 targeted edits to `unsloth/save.py`

| # | Location | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Line ~27 | `import json` (not yet present) |
| 2 | Line 29 | Add `get_lora_parameters` to the existing `.kernels` import |
| 3 | Lines 48–54 | Add `"unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx"` to `__all__` |
| 4 | After line ~2561 | New `_LoRAONNXModule(nn.Module)` — registers A/B as float32 buffers; forward = `(x @ A.T) @ B.T * scaling` |
| 5 | After line ~2561 | New `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(self, save_directory, opset_version=17)` function |
| 6 | Line ~2664 (non-vision branch of `patch_saving_functions`) | `model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)` |
| 7 | Line ~2674 (vision branch) | Same binding — LoRA is architecture-agnostic, unlike GGUF |

**Key design choices:**
- Uses the existing `isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear))` pattern (line 162) to find adapter layers
- Uses `get_lora_parameters` (already in `kernels/__init__.py` line 38) to cleanly extract A/B/scaling from quantised and QAT layers alike
- A/B cast to float32 for broadest ONNX runtime compatibility
- `peft_config` access is guarded with `getattr(self, "peft_config", {})` since it's not currently referenced anywhere in `save.py`
- `metadata.json` stores scaling per-module to handle models with per-layer rank/alpha (DoRA, rank-stabilised LoRA)

        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (

def print_quantization_methods():
